title: Impact of number of bone metastases on cancer control outcomes in mHSPC, independent of the axial-skeleton CHAARTED definition.
abstract: Patient and treatment stratification for metastatic hormone-sensitive prostate cancer (mHSPC) is usually based on metastatic burden according to CHAARTED criteria, classifying high-volume disease as at least 4 bone metastases, one outside the axial skeleton, or one visceral metastasis. We aimed to examine the impact of the number of bone metastases independently of CHAARTED criteria.
